witnesses.description: Exact rational inner-product witnesses in Q^2.

checks.claim: The standard Gram matrix on Q^2 gives the listed dot products and sample bilinearity rows.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes dot products, vector addition, scalar multiplication, and two sample bilinearity equations.

checks.claim: The weighted Gram matrix [[2,1],[1,2]] is symmetric positive definite.
checks.notes: The validator checks symmetry and Sylvester leading principal minors 2 and 3.

checks.claim: The fixed vectors u=[1,2] and v=[3,-1] satisfy Cauchy-Schwarz under the standard inner product.
The projection of [2,3] onto span([1,1]) is [5/2,5/2] with orthogonal residual.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the coefficient, projection, residual orthogonality, and norm decomposition.

checks.claim: The residual from projecting [2,3] onto span([1,1]) has inner product 1 with the basis vector.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: Exact projection replay computes <residual,basis> = 0, while the malformed row claims 1; the source QF_LRA artifact isolates that final equality conflict for Farkas checking.

checks.claim: The listed Gram-Schmidt residuals turn [1,1],[1,0] into an orthogonal rational basis.
checks.notes: The validator checks projection subtraction, orthogonality, and nonzero determinants for both bases.

checks.claim: The matrix diag(1,-1) is not a valid real inner product because [0,1] has negative norm square.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the negative quadratic form and the negative determinant; the nonzero-vector positivity conflict is checked through QF_LRA/Farkas evidence.

checks.claim: General inner-product and Hilbert-space theory requires a proof-assistant route.

data.target_theorem_shape: General Cauchy-Schwarz, Gram-Schmidt, orthonormal bases, Riesz representation, projection theorem, Hilbert-space completeness, adjoints, and spectral theorem statements over arbitrary real/complex inner-product or Hilbert spaces.
data.future_checker: Lean/mathlib linear algebra, analysis, and Hilbert-space libraries plus Axeyum evidence import for finite exact subgoals.

checks.notes: The finite rows are exact rational checks; the general theory remains proof-assistant territory.
